metadata.title: AI Anomaly Detection: Teaching Machines to See Smoke Before Flame

beats.narration_text: Kia ora — this is Liam, in for Bear. Based on the essay 'Why Your Factory Knows It's About to Break (Before You Do)' by Aditi Shinde, for NortheasternISE / Humanitarians AI. How do factories know a machine is about to break? AI anomaly detection compresses sensor data into a compact mathematical representation and then tries to reconstruct the original. When reconstruction fails, the AI flags the anomaly. Context-aware, not threshold-blind. The pharmaceutical HEPA filter was flagged 42 days before it would have caused a sterility failure.

props.segment: AI Anomaly Detection: Teaching Machines to See Smoke Before Flame
props.command: How do factories know a machine is about to break?
output: Autoencoder: compress → reconstruct. Reconstruction failure = anomaly signal.
Context-aware: 70% CPU normal Monday; catastrophic at 3am Saturday.
Pharma HEPA filter flagged 42 days before sterility failure.

beats.narration_text: Compare two monitoring approaches on the same underlying data. Static threshold monitoring: you set a threshold for CPU temperature, vibration, or pressure. The metric stays below the threshold — sensor reads normal. Then it spikes and crosses the threshold — alarm fires. For slow degradations — gradual bearing wear, progressive insulation breakdown — the metric approaches the threshold over weeks. The alarm is late. Reconstruction error monitoring: the autoencoder is trained on normal data. It learns what normal reconstruction looks like. As the component degrades, its patterns diverge from normal. The reconstruction error — the gap between input and reconstructed output — rises gradually, tracking the degradation. The alarm is early.

manim.description: Two monitoring lines for the same underlying degradation data. Left: threshold monitoring — flat line, then sudden spike crossing threshold at failure. Alarm fires at failure. Right: reconstruction error — gradual rise beginning weeks before failure, crosses alert threshold early. Both on same time axis. 'Detection window' labeled between early alert and failure.

beats.act: false alarm rate and detection window
beats.narration_text: Two metrics determine which monitoring approach to use: false alarm rate and detection window. Static threshold: low false alarm rate when nothing is degrading — the metric is either under or over the line. High false alarm rate when normal variation takes metrics near the threshold during peak load. Detection window: essentially zero — the alarm fires when the threshold is crossed, which may be simultaneous with or after the failure event. Autoencoder monitoring: false alarm rate is managed by the reconstruction error threshold, tunable. Detection window: days to weeks, because reconstruction error rises with degradation. The pharmaceutical HEPA filter example: 42 days of rising reconstruction error before failure. Static threshold would have sounded zero seconds of advance warning.

animated_deck.description: Two-column comparison: static threshold vs. autoencoder monitoring. Rows: false alarm rate (threshold: high during peak load; autoencoder: tunable), detection window (threshold: ~0 days; autoencoder: days to weeks). HEPA filter: 42-day detection window annotated. All metrics favor autoencoder.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two metrics determine which monitoring approach to use: false alarm rate and detection window. Static threshold: low false alarm rate when nothing is degrading

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. Static threshold monitoring sounds an alarm when a metric exceeds a limit — it stays silent during slow degradations. A CPU at 70% is normal Monday morning and catastrophic at 3 AM Saturday; context-blind thresholds cannot know that. The autoencoder compresses and tries to reconstruct; reconstruction failure is the anomaly signal. The pharmaceutical HEPA filter was flagged 42 days before it would have caused a sterility failure. Train on normal. Measure the gap between input and reconstruction. The gap is the anomaly.

props.artifactTitle: AI Anomaly Detection: Teaching Machines to See Smoke Before Flame
artifactLines: Static threshold: silent during slow degradations, fires at or after failure.
Context-blind: 70% CPU normal at 9am, catastrophic at 3am — threshold can't know.
Autoencoder: compress → reconstruct. Reconstruction failure = anomaly signal.
HEPA filter: flagged 42 days before sterility failure. Static threshold: 0 days.

beats.narration_text: Your turn. Here is the prompt — read it with me. 'Train an autoencoder on a time-series sensor dataset. Plot the reconstruction error over time. Can you identify anomalies before they cross a traditional threshold? What's the detection window advantage?' The detection window advantage is the number — how many days or hours of advance warning does reconstruction error give you compared to a threshold alarm? Train on a normal period, then plot reconstruction error across a period that includes an anomaly or failure event. Liam, in for Bear.
